source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Four identical coherent monochromatic wave sources A, B, C, D, as shown in Fig. 4.2 produce waves of the same wavelength $\lambda$. Two receivers $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$ are at great (but equal) distances from $B$. Which receiver can tell which source, B or D, has been turned off?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: $R_{2}$ can determine the on-off state of the sources B and D
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object representation rather than a file path or URL

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object representation rather than a file path or URL

image_dependency: 题干显式写有 'as shown in Fig. 4.2'，关键几何关系依赖图示。
requires_image=true 且 text_dominant=false，说明文本本身不足以独立支撑解题。
需要区分接收器 R1、R2 与源 B、D 的相对位置和路径关系，这属于强视觉/空间依赖。
multi_step: 需要结合波源相干、等波长以及接收器到各源的相对路径关系进行推断，不是直接读图即可作答。
问题本质上要比较“关闭 B”与“关闭 D”对不同接收器干涉结果的影响，至少包含条件组合与比较两步。
答案为单一接收器，但推理通常需要图形信息与物理干涉原理联合使用，因此多步性中等偏上。
verifiability: answer_text 给出明确唯一结论 'R2'，输出形式清晰。
题目目标是二选一式判断（R1 或 R2），后续核验可基于标准答案或基于图中路径差规则检查。
registry_passed=true，且答案与图像资产均已登记，支持后续样本级核验。

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Four identical coherent monochromatic wave sources A, B, C, D, as shown in Fig. 4.2 produce waves of the same wavelength $\lambda$. Two receivers $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$ are at great (but equal) distances from $B$. Which receiver can tell which source, B or D, has been turned off?
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text: $R_{2}$ can determine the on-off state of the sources B and D
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object representation rather than a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: From the above, we can see that $I_{1}$ remains the same whether the sources $B$ and $D$ are on or off. Hence $R_{1}$ cannot determine the on-off state of $B$ and $D$. On the other hand, the intensity of $I_{2}$ differs for the three cases above so the strength of the signal received by $R_{2}$ can determine the on-off state of the sources $B$ and $D$.

problem_main_record.reasoning_chain: From the above, we can see that $I_{1}$ remains the same whether the sources $B$ and $D$ are on or off. Hence $R_{1}$ cannot determine the on-off state of $B$ and $D$. On the other hand, the intensity of $I_{2}$ differs for the three cases above so the strength of the signal received by $R_{2}$ can determine the on-off state of the sources $B$ and $D$.
